## Overview

**v0.4.3 — macOS Hanja conversion UX + reliability cleanup.**

This release packages the follow-ups from the repository audit after v0.4.2.
The headline change is macOS `NSTextInputClient` reconversion support: Korean
Hanja / kanji conversion via Option+Return can now query committed text,
in-progress Hangul commits, and cursor rectangles from the active terminal row
and tab rename editor.
It also handles the common Korean flow where Option+Return is pressed while a
Hangul syllable is still in pre-edit: TildaZ commits the Hangul, opens the
candidate list immediately, keeps the original Hangul visible while candidates
are selected, and replaces it only when a candidate is accepted.

No config schema changes, no migration, and no ghostty dependency update.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.4.3-win-x64.zip` | Unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. Unsigned — same AV / SmartScreen handling as v0.4.2. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.4.3-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: same ad-hoc-signing setup as v0.4.2 — right-click
`TildaZ.app` → Open, or `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine
/Applications/TildaZ.app`. No re-grant of permissions if you already ran
v0.4.2.

## Migration from v0.4.2

None. Same config schema, same defaults.

## What's new since v0.4.2

### macOS IME reconversion

- **macOS Option+Return Hanja / kanji reconversion**
  ([#166](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/166),
  [#190](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/190)). The macOS host now
  implements the AppKit reconversion callbacks that IMEs use to inspect and
  replace text: `selectedRange`, `markedRange`,
  `attributedSubstringForProposedRange:actualRange:`,
  `characterIndexForPoint:`, `firstRectForCharacterRange:actualRange:`, and
  `insertText:replacementRange:`. The implementation builds snapshots from the
  active terminal cursor row and the tab-rename buffer, so candidate windows
  can anchor at the right screen rectangle and replacements can be applied
  safely.

- **Pre-edit Hanja conversion opens the candidate list immediately**
  ([#190](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/190)). Pressing
  Option+Return while a Hangul syllable is still composing now commits that
  Hangul, opens the Hanja candidate list in the same action, and keeps the
  original Hangul visible while the list is open. Accepting a candidate replaces
  the Hangul with the chosen Hanja. Esc, candidate cancel, or focus loss leaves
  the original Hangul in place. This behavior works in both the terminal row
  and tab rename.

- **Candidate navigation and popup layering were tightened**
  ([#190](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/190)). Arrow keys stay with
  the IME candidate list while it is open instead of being forwarded as terminal
  escape sequences, and the macOS drop-down window is lowered only when AppKit
  asks for the candidate anchor rectangle. This keeps the candidate panel
  visible without changing normal drop-down window behavior. Arbitrary terminal
  ranges outside the immediate cursor row still fall back to plain insertion
  instead of guessing at unsafe edits.

### Cross-platform fixes and cleanup

- **macOS empty shell validation now matches the shared config policy**
  ([#177](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/177)). Empty shell strings
  are rejected consistently instead of slipping through one host path.
- **macOS renderer color policy aligned with Windows**
  ([#178](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/178)). Renderer color
  decisions now follow the same theme-derived behavior across both hosts.
- **ConPTY shell command truncation removed**
  ([#179](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/179)). Windows shell command
  construction no longer relies on a fixed short buffer.
- **macOS PTY teardown no longer signals an already-exited child**
  ([#180](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/180)). Exit tracking avoids
  unnecessary signals during cleanup.
- **Pre-edit width calculation shared across hosts**
  ([#181](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/181)). IME overlay sizing now
  uses the same helper in terminal cells and tab rename.
- **User-visible messages and dialogs now go through the shared layer**
  ([#182](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/182)). Panic, patch-failure,
  config, About, and limit dialogs are routed through `messages.zig` and
  `dialog.zig`.
- **Per-tab interaction comments updated**
  ([#183](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/183)). Stale ownership
  comments were corrected after the interaction-state refactor.
- **ConPTY environment restore buffer grows dynamically**
  ([#184](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/184)). Long environment
  blocks no longer depend on a small fixed restore buffer.
- **Font fallback chain limit centralized and documented**
  ([#185](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/185)). The maximum font chain
  length is now a shared constant (`MAX_FONT_CHAIN = 8`) and the limit is
  documented in the spec.
- **macOS auto-start plist writes are skipped when unchanged**
  ([#190](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/190)). When `auto_start` is
  already registered with the same LaunchAgent content, TildaZ no longer
  rewrites `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.tildaz.app.plist` on every launch. This
  avoids unnecessary timestamp churn and reduces the chance of macOS surfacing
  background-activity notices when nothing actually changed.

## Known issues

- The known issues from v0.4.2 / v0.4.1 still apply, except the old macOS
  Hanja reconversion limitation is fixed by
  [#166](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/166) and
  [#190](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/190).
- macOS emoji picker anchoring is still a system limitation for custom terminal
  views; see the spec appendix for the current behavior and workarounds.

## Verification

- [x] `zig build test`
- [x] `zig build`
- [x] `zig build -Dtarget=x86_64-windows`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] macOS manual: with Korean IME, type Hangul in the terminal row, press
      Option+Return during pre-edit, confirm the candidate popup opens, arrow
      navigation works, Esc preserves the original Hangul, and accepting a
      candidate replaces it with Hanja.
- [x] macOS manual: repeat the same Option+Return flow while renaming a tab.
- [x] Windows manual: launch the local Windows build, confirm ConPTY tabs start
      normally and the IME path is not regressed.
